title: Temperature Converter
description:
Write a program that converts a temperature from Fahrenheit to Celsius.

The formula is: C = (F - 32) × 5.0 / 9.0

Given `fahrenheit = 98.6`, calculate and print the Celsius equivalent.
hints: Remember to use 5.0 instead of 5 to avoid integer division
Store the result in a double variable, then print it

title: Area of a Triangle
description:
Write a program that calculates the area of a triangle.

The formula is: area = (base × height) / 2.0

Given `base = 13` and `height = 7`, calculate and print the area. Make sure you get a decimal result!
hints: If you divide two ints, you get integer division — the decimal is lost!
Use 2.0 instead of 2 in your division, or cast one of the values to double

title: Tip Calculator
description:
You went out to eat and your bill is $84.60. You want to leave an 18% tip.

Write a program that:
1. Calculates the tip amount
2. Calculates the total bill (original + tip)
3. Splits the total evenly between 3 friends
4. Prints all three values on separate lines

Use the variables provided. Round nothing — just let Java do its thing.
hints: To calculate the tip: bill * tipPercent / 100
Total is bill + tip. Per person is total / friends.
When you divide a double by an int, Java automatically promotes the int to a double, so you'll get a decimal result.

title: Digit Extractor
description:
Write a program that takes a 4-digit integer and prints each digit on a separate line, from left to right.

Given `number = 4927`, your output should be:
4
9
2
7

**Challenge:** You can ONLY use integer division (/) and modulo (%). Do NOT convert to a String!
hints: To get the thousands digit: number / 1000
To get the hundreds digit: (number / 100) % 10 — dividing by 100 shifts it over, then % 10 grabs just that digit

title: The Swap Challenge
description:
You have two integer variables, `a` and `b`. Swap their values so that `a` holds the original value of `b`, and `b` holds the original value of `a`.

**Rules:**
- You CANNOT just write `a = 7; b = 3;` — that's cheating!
- You need to figure out the logic to swap ANY two values.
- You'll need a third variable to help.

After swapping, print both values to verify.

**Bonus challenge (hard):** Can you swap them WITHOUT using a third variable? (Hint: use addition and subtraction.)
hints: Think of it like swapping the contents of two cups — you need a third empty cup to hold one while you pour.
Create a temporary variable: int temp = a; then set a = b; then set b = temp;
Bonus (no temp variable): a = a + b; then b = a - b; then a = a - b; Think about why this works!
